story.iWant: to monitor sensors in real-time during plan execution and immediately avoid obstacles or adjust paths
story.soThat: collisions are prevented proactively and replanning frequency is reduced

tasks.task: Integrate Ollama for Quick Detour Decisions
Update VerifierAgent to Adjust Tolerance for Reactive Logs

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: **Level 1 - Emergency Stop (Rule-based)** - Lidar less than 0.15m detected triggers immediate stop (reaction time less than 0.001s) - Return action failure to trigger Verifier replanning - No AI involvement - pure rule-based decision - Test: Unit test validates emergency stop logic

**Level 2 - Quick Detour (Ollama AI)** - Lidar between 0.15m and 0.5m detected triggers Ollama tinyllama for detour decision (avg 0.68s, P90 1.03s from Story 3.0) - Path adjusted via x, y, speed parameters and execution continues - No replanning needed - time savings - Test: Unit test validates Ollama detour decision integration
**Level 3 - Normal Execution** - Lidar greater than 0.5m triggers execution of plan as designed - No reactive intervention - Test: Unit test validates normal execution path

**reactive_log Recording and Propagation** - All reactive interventions recorded with timestamp, type, reason, action_taken, sensor_state - Stored in RobotState.reactive_log field - Passed to Verifier for tolerance expansion (0.1m to 0.3m for reactive adjustments) - Test: Integration test validates log propagation and Verifier tolerance adjustment

**Real-time Execution Guarantee** - check_and_react() called every 64ms - Non-blocking with return time less than 10ms - Multi-agent flow maintained (Planner/Actor/Verifier unchanged) - Test: Performance test validates timing constraints

**Test Coverage** - Emergency stop test (unit test) - Ollama detour test (unit test) - reactive_log propagation test (integration test) - Verifier tolerance adjustment test (integration test) - E2E scenario (detour to success) - Minimum 90% code coverage for reactive module

**Performance Benchmarking and Validation** - Execute 50+ missions with reactive controller enabled - Measure metrics: collision rate, replanning frequency, mission completion time, success rate - Compare against Epic 2 baseline (Story 2.5 metrics) - Validate goals: 93% collision reduction, 95% success rate, 91% replanning reduction - Generate benchmark report: docs/epic3_benchmark_report.md

doc.title: LLM_ROBOT_2 Architecture - Epic 3 Section 11.3
doc.snippet: Defines 3-level decision architecture (CRITICAL/MODERATE/NORMAL), HybridReactiveController class design with check_and_react() method, ActorAgent integration pattern with 64ms check cycle, and VerifierAgent tolerance adjustment logic (0.1m to 0.3m expansion).

doc.title: Epic Breakdown - Story 3.1 Definition
doc.snippet: 7 acceptance criteria covering emergency stop, Ollama detour, normal execution, reactive_log propagation, real-time guarantees, test coverage, and performance benchmarking. Expected impact: 93% collision reduction, 91% replanning reduction, 95% success rate.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Ollama integration specifications, performance targets (reactive check less than 10ms, Ollama detour ~680ms avg), graceful degradation patterns, and Epic 3 success metrics.

doc.title: Ollama Setup Guide (Story 3.0 Deliverable)
doc.snippet: Ollama client initialization patterns, JSON parsing with markdown code block handling, TinyLlama performance metrics (680ms avg, 1027ms P90), and graceful degradation strategy established in Story 3.0.

doc.title: Story 3.0: Ollama Setup & Validation (Completed)
doc.snippet: Ollama infrastructure validated and ready. Key learnings: TinyLlama wraps JSON in markdown code blocks (strip before parsing), performance validated (680ms avg, 1027ms P90), avoid Unicode emojis (Windows cp949 errors), graceful degradation path documented.

artifact.reason: Must integrate HybridReactiveController initialization and call check_and_react() every 64ms during _execute_move(). Add get_reactive_log() method to retrieve intervention list and update RobotState before returning to Verifier.
Must add reactive_log: List[Dict[str, Any]] = Field(default_factory=list) field to store reactive interventions. This is the data structure that propagates reactive context from Actor to Verifier.
Must modify verification logic to check RobotState.reactive_log and expand tolerance from 0.1m to 0.3m when reactive interventions are present. Implements tolerance adjustment per AC #4.
Source of sensor data (Lidar, GPS, IMU, Camera) that HybridReactiveController will consume. Provides get_sensor_data() method with filtered readings established in Story 2.2.

Multi-agent coordination layer. Reactive controller operates within Actor execution, preserving Planner → Actor → Verifier flow. No modifications needed but must understand flow preservation requirement.
Story 2.3 safety constraint system. Reactive controller emergency stop (Level 1 CRITICAL) complements but does not replace SafetyValidator. Both systems work together for defense-in-depth.
Story 3.0 test patterns for Ollama integration. Reference for mocking Ollama client, JSON parsing validation, and performance testing patterns. 5/5 tests passed validating infrastructure readiness.

constraints.constraint: **Multi-agent Flow Preservation**: Reactive controller operates WITHIN Actor execution. Planner → Actor → Verifier flow remains unchanged. Do NOT bypass or replace the multi-agent architecture.

**Real-time Guarantees**: check_and_react() must return in less than 10ms (95th percentile). Use non-blocking patterns, avoid I/O in critical path. 64ms check cycle is non-negotiable for real-time responsiveness.

**Ollama Integration Pattern (from Story 3.0)**: Use ollama.Client with host="http://localhost:11434", model="tinyllama". Strip markdown code blocks before JSON parsing (TinyLlama behavior). Handle ConnectionError, TimeoutException, JSONDecodeError with fallback to CRITICAL mode.

**reactive_log Structure**: Each entry must include: timestamp (ISO format), type (CRITICAL/MODERATE/NORMAL), reason (string), action_taken (dict), sensor_state (dict). This is a List[Dict[str, Any]] stored in RobotState.

**Test Coverage Standard (from Epic 1-2)**: Minimum 90% code coverage for src/reactive/ module. Unit tests for each decision level, integration tests for data flow, E2E tests for full scenarios, benchmarks for performance validation (50+ missions).
**Defense-in-Depth**: Emergency stop (Level 1 CRITICAL) is first line of defense. SafetyValidator (Story 2.3) provides second layer. Both systems must coexist and complement each other.

**Ollama Failure Handling**: If Ollama unavailable or fails, fall back to CRITICAL mode (emergency stop rules only). Log: "Ollama unavailable, falling back to rules-only reactive mode". System continues operating with reduced capabilities.

interface.name: HybridReactiveController.check_and_react()
interface.signature:
def check_and_react(self, sensor_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> ReactiveDecision: """ 3-level reactive decision logic.
Args: sensor_data: Dict with keys 'lidar', 'gps', 'imu', 'camera'
Returns: ReactiveDecision with intervention_type, action, metadata
Timing: Must return in less than 10ms (non-blocking) """

interface.name: ActorAgent._execute_move_with_reactive()
interface.signature:
def _execute_move_with_reactive(self, action: RobotAction, duration: float) -> RobotState: """ Execute move action with 64ms reactive check cycle.
Args: action: RobotAction with type, parameters duration: Execution duration in seconds
Returns: RobotState with reactive_log populated
Integration: Calls HybridReactiveController.check_and_react() every 64ms """

interface.name: VerifierAgent.verify_with_reactive_tolerance()
interface.signature:
def verify_with_reactive_tolerance(self, robot_state: RobotState) -> VerificationResult: """ Verify action result with reactive-aware tolerance.
Args: robot_state: RobotState with optional reactive_log
Returns: VerificationResult with success, message
Logic: If reactive_log non-empty, expand tolerance 0.1m → 0.3m """

interface.name: RobotState.reactive_log

tests.standards: Testing framework: pytest (established in Epic 1-2). Test categories: Unit tests for isolated logic (mock Ollama, validate decision logic), Integration tests for data flow (Actor → Verifier reactive_log propagation), E2E tests for full scenarios (emergency stop → replanning, detour → success), Benchmarks for performance validation (50+ missions, compare Epic 2 baseline). Code coverage target: greater than 90% for src/reactive/ module. All tests must pass before story completion.
tests.locations: tests/test_reactive_controller.py - Unit tests tests/integration/test_reactive_integration.py - Integration tests tests/e2e/test_reactive_e2e.py - End-to-end tests tests/benchmarks/test_reactive_benchmarks.py - Performance benchmarks

ideas.idea: **Test Emergency Stop Logic (Unit)** - Setup: Mock sensor_data with lidar_min = 0.12m (less than 0.15m threshold) - Execute: HybridReactiveController.check_and_react(sensor_data) - Assert: intervention_type = "CRITICAL", action = emergency_stop, reaction_time less than 0.001s - Coverage: AC #1 Level 1 CRITICAL decision path

**Test Ollama Detour Decision (Unit, Mocked)** - Setup: Mock sensor_data with lidar_min = 0.3m (0.15m less than x less than 0.5m), mock Ollama client response with DetourPlan JSON - Execute: HybridReactiveController.check_and_react(sensor_data) - Assert: intervention_type = "MODERATE", action contains detour coordinates, Ollama called with correct prompt - Coverage: AC #2 Level 2 MODERATE decision path with AI integration

**Test Normal Execution (Unit)** - Setup: Mock sensor_data with lidar_min = 0.8m (greater than 0.5m threshold) - Execute: HybridReactiveController.check_and_react(sensor_data) - Assert: intervention_type = "NONE", no action taken - Coverage: AC #3 Level 3 NORMAL path (no reactive intervention)

**Test reactive_log Propagation (Integration)** - Setup: ActorAgent with HybridReactiveController, trigger MODERATE intervention during move - Execute: ActorAgent._execute_move_with_reactive() with obstacle scenario - Assert: RobotState.reactive_log populated with timestamp, type, reason, action_taken, sensor_state - Assert: VerifierAgent receives RobotState with reactive_log, tolerance expanded to 0.3m - Coverage: AC #4 data flow from Actor to Verifier

**Test Real-time Performance (Performance)** - Setup: HybridReactiveController with actual Ollama connection - Execute: 1000 iterations of check_and_react() with varied sensor data - Measure: check_and_react() latency for CRITICAL (rule-based) and NORMAL (no-op) modes - Assert: P95 latency less than 10ms for non-Ollama modes, validate 64ms check cycle feasibility - Coverage: AC #5 real-time execution guarantee

**Test Ollama Graceful Degradation (Integration)** - Setup: HybridReactiveController with Ollama service stopped (ConnectionError scenario) - Execute: check_and_react() with MODERATE trigger (0.15m less than lidar less than 0.5m) - Assert: Falls back to CRITICAL mode (emergency stop), logs "Ollama unavailable, falling back to rules-only" - Coverage: Constraint: graceful-degradation pattern

**Test E2E Scenario: Emergency Stop → Replanning → Success** - Setup: Full Orchestrator with reactive-enabled ActorAgent, obstacle at 0.1m - Execute: execute_mission() with move_forward command - Assert: Emergency stop triggered, action failure returned, Verifier triggers replanning, alternative plan succeeds - Coverage: AC #6 E2E test, validates multi-agent flow preservation with reactive layer

**Test E2E Scenario: Ollama Detour → Continue → Success** - Setup: Full Orchestrator with reactive-enabled ActorAgent, obstacle at 0.3m - Execute: execute_mission() with move_forward command - Assert: Ollama detour triggered, path adjusted, mission continues without replanning, reactive_log populated, Verifier tolerance expanded, mission succeeds - Coverage: AC #6 E2E test, validates Level 2 MODERATE AI-powered detour in full system

**Benchmark: 50+ Missions with Reactive Controller** - Setup: Test suite with 50+ varied missions (different obstacle configurations, mission types) - Execute: Run all missions with reactive controller enabled, measure collision rate, replanning frequency, mission completion time, success rate - Compare: Against Epic 2 baseline metrics from Story 2.5 evaluation - Assert: Collision reduction greater than or equal to 93%, success rate greater than or equal to 95%, replanning reduction greater than or equal to 91% - Generate: docs/epic3_benchmark_report.md with tables, charts, analysis - Coverage: AC #7 performance benchmarking and validation
